purpose: Map the smallest repository surfaces a Pi-backed agent needs for verified installation, low-noise launch, intake, bounded orchestration, validation, artifact production, and handoff.

directories.purpose: Canonical manifests, schemas, workflows, registries, fixtures, and proof policy.
Scoped agent procedures loaded by Pi through project-local settings; no product behavior belongs only in prose.
Project-local Pi settings and reviewed modular resources; project trust is required and never bypassed.
Pi installation, launch, status, adapter maps, workflows, schemas, and artifact contracts.
Opt-in local validation hooks; never installed implicitly.
Repository-level deterministic validators and read-only probes.
Dependency-free structural, security, and behavioral contracts.
English operator guidance, status, gaps, risks, and proof ceilings.

commands.name: Install pinned Pi
commands.effect: global exact-version CLI package installation; no global Pi configuration or authentication mutation
commands.proof: installer command and exact executable/version readback

commands.name: Verify pinned Pi
commands.proof: Node/npm readiness, package identity, executable path, and exact version

commands.name: Start AgentSwitchboard Pi
commands.effect: operator-authorized Pi runtime; telemetry and version checks disabled by default

commands.name: Pi dependency-free contracts
commands.proof: JSON, workflow, installer, launcher, trust, and privacy semantics

knownTraps: Do not describe provider or model access as universally free merely because the Pi CLI is free and MIT-licensed.
Do not infer provider readiness, privacy, model delivery, or extension safety from successful Pi installation.
Do not bypass the Pi project-trust prompt or silently mutate global Pi settings, credentials, providers, or models.
Do not execute third-party extension snippets until the source, exact version, permissions, and integrity are reviewed.
Enforce one writer per branch; architect, builder, validator, and adjudicator roles must not share write authority.
Do not fuse unattributed outputs or hide unresolved divergence.
Do not run autovalidation without attempt, wall-clock, token, cancellation, and no-progress bounds.
Do not store prompts, raw model output, credentials, sessions, or local endpoint evidence in tracked artifacts.
proofCeiling: Verified upstream identity and exact-version installer/launcher contracts, project-local skill wiring, low-noise controls, repository map, and deterministic cross-platform validation. No provider login, model response, extension execution, network privacy, opinion-fusion delivery, autovalidation success, or operator acceptance proof.
